items.title: Synology outlines storage and surveillance roadmap
items.summary: Businesses will get cheaper storage and wider backup coverage as Synology adds SATA-based systems, object storage and AI tools to its roadmap.

items.title: Sophos & Rubrik launch Microsoft 365 backup service
items.summary: Security teams can now restore Microsoft 365 data from ransomware or deletion within Sophos Central, reducing reliance on separate backup tools.

items.title: PhishByte warns spear phishing drives payment fraud losses
items.summary: Targeted email scams are pushing payment redirection fraud losses higher as Australian firms lose more than AUD $166.8 million.

items.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
items.summary: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.

items.title: Soap Box: Detection and response in the AI age
items.summary: In this sponsored Soap Box edition of the Risky Business podcast Patrick Gray chats with Edward Wu, founder of Dropzone, about what AI is doing to detection, response and the SOC more generally. items.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
items.summary: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.

items.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
items.summary: Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.

items.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
items.summary: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.

items.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
items.summary: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.

items.title: Companies Are Using Reddit to Manipulate ChatGPT and Google AI Search
items.summary: Peptide companies have been doing AI-engine optimization by spamming the biohackers subreddit to manipulate ChatGPT and Google.

items.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
items.summary: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.

items.title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
items.summary: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools.

items.summary: Over 116,000 Minecraft systems infected in WeedHack malware campaign BleepingComputer

items.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
items.summary: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.

items.title: DriveSurge Hijacks Thousands of Sites for ClickFix, FakeUpdate Attacks
items.summary: A sneaky, wide-scale IAB operation uses a malicious traffic distribution system (TDS) to redirect visitors of trusted websites to ones that deliver malware.

items.title: China Uses Dual-Method Cyberattack on Czech Orgs
items.summary: China is stealing data from high-value targets via a sneaky, double-layer spear-phishing campaign that includes the Azureveil malware.

items.title: Anthropic to Open Mythos AI to EU's ENISA
items.summary: The European security agency's entry to Project Glasswing is the result of "strong bilateral cooperation" between the European Commission and Anthropic.

items.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
items.summary: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.

items.summary: WordPress malware campaign hides payloads in Steam profiles BleepingComputer

items.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
items.summary: Exploiting the PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability requires certain conditions, but adversaries have done so in two attack waves that started in mid-May.

items.title: 1,000 Data Breaches Later, the Disclosure Lag is Worse Than Ever
items.summary: Today, I loaded the 1,000th data breach into Have I Been Pwned. Reflecting on that milestone number, I pondered how to mark the occasion in writing, and what immediately came to mind was a very simple question: why is it still needed? Especially considering the emergence of privacy regulations

items.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
items.summary: Researchers discover an exploit chain combining over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities that could have compromised a popular automation service.

items.title: 'The Com' Cyberattacks Support Violence & Sexploitation
items.summary: Your organization's security failures have consequences for everyone else as well since this criminal gang uses its cyber winnings to support more violent and widespread crimes.

items.title: Dutch Raid Fails to Dent Russian Bulletproof Host
items.summary: Dutch law enforcement seized 800 servers and arrested two operators of THE.Hosting but left the hosting provider's core IP address space intact.

items.title: Focus on Cyber Insurance: How Quantifying Risk Is Reshaping Security
items.summary: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how cyber insurance is forcing organizations to quantify risk, what's covered (and what's not), and why this could be the best thing to happen to cybersecurity.

items.title: BTMOB RAT Spreads Across Brazil, LatAm via MaaS Model
items.summary: An advanced remote access Trojan is propagating online. Notably, it's delivered via an operator licensing model and features a no-code malware-development interface.

items.title: Nordic CISOs Handle Rising Cyber Threats Remarkably Well
items.summary: Artificial intelligence notwithstanding, the vast majority of CISOs in northern Europe say they're facing no more serious cyberattacks than they did two years ago.

items.title: Australians more concerned about privacy as trust in AI remains low, OAIC survey finds
items.summary: A large majority of Australians say they are more concerned about privacy than they were five years ago, according to a new survey released by the Office of the Australian [...]

items.title: Latin American Cybercriminals Hoover Up Government Data
items.summary: A purported leak exposing 5.8 million records of Uruguayan citizens is the latest incident where cybercriminals targeted government agencies to monetize citizen data.

items.title: AI-Assisted Exploit Development Outpaces Scanner Detection
items.summary: Attackers are using AI to dramatically reduce the time they need to develop a working exploit for a CVE, according to new research.

items.title: Feeding Frenzy: 'Megalodon' Malware Infects Thousands of GitHub Repos
items.summary: In just six hours, the campaign quietly pushed thousands of malicious commits to more than 5,500 GitHub repositories, stealing credentials, developer secrets, and more.

items.title: State Cyber Leaders Push Congress for More Funding, Support
items.summary: A recent congressional hearing highlighted how states are reeling from federal cutbacks to important cybergrants and information-sharing initiatives amid damaging attacks to critical infrastructure.

items.title: Shai-Hulud Hackers TeamPCP: Lucky or Skilled?
items.summary: TeamPCP, the cybercrime group behind later waves of the Shai-Hulud worm, has done significant damage to the open source ecosystem. But it's not necessarily due to skill alone.

items.title: Microsoft Issues Out-of-Band SharePoint Patch
items.summary: SharePoint often gives access to the keys of the kingdom, something attackers and defenders understand all too well.

items.summary: Today, we welcome the 45th government onboarded to Have I Been Pwned’s free gov service: Bhutan. items.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.summary: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union.
